nodes.label: Mechanistic Interpretability
nodes.description: Reverse-engineering neural networks to understand how internal circuits and features produce model behaviour, enabling targeted safety interventions.

nodes.label: AI Control
nodes.description: Designing oversight protocols and containment strategies that keep AI systems operating within intended bounds, even under distributional shift.

nodes.label: Deception Detection
nodes.description: Identifying when AI systems produce outputs that are strategically misleading, and building robust methods to surface hidden model intentions.

nodes.label: AI Moral Status
nodes.description: Investigating whether and when AI systems might warrant moral consideration, and what legal and ethical frameworks should apply — the subject of my AAAI 2026 paper.

nodes.label: Co-Alignment
nodes.description: Moving beyond one-way alignment to study how humans and AI systems can iteratively converge on shared values through mutual adaptation.

nodes.label: Technical AI Governance
nodes.description: Bridging the gap between technical AI safety research and policy implementation — translating capability assessments into enforceable regulatory standards.

nodes.label: Mens Rea Adjudication
nodes.description: Examining how criminal law concepts of intent and culpability apply when an AI system causes harm — who bears responsibility, and how mechanistic evidence informs that judgment.

nodes.label: AI Evaluations
nodes.description: Developing rigorous benchmarks and red-teaming methodologies that measure dangerous capabilities and alignment properties before and after deployment.
